calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-01-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Rosie Ortiz.

Mr. Huysentruyt stated that it did not occur to him to lower the bond. The matter was taken under submission. The Court informed Counsel that there may be a reduction in fees due to failure to reduce the bond.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-15-2010 Continued To Nov-29-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-04-2010 Continued To Nov-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Rosie Ortiz.

The Court stated that at the last hearing that they had talked about the deficiencies, that it looked at the Amended Account filed 10/1/10, that it still suffers from some of the same dificiencies as the original account, and inquired as to where the $700.00 annuity monies that had been paid out for a number of years had gone; who received it. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that the money went to Mexico to the decedent's account,that there was a probate proceeding in Mexico, that the funds have been distributed to her children, and that is why they are closing the Estate. The Court stated that it wanted Mr. Huysentruyt to file a supplement stating all the information that he just provided and that it wanted something from the insurance company regarding the annuity as well. Mr. Huysentruyt stated that initially when the structured settlement was set up there was a payment that went directly to an account at Bank of America, that the decedent moved back to Mexico, and that when she did she made arrangements for automatic transfers of the payments from Bank of America to her account in Mexico. The Court stated that it wants it all laid out, that once the distribution takes place it wants something from the insurance company, and that it wants to know if they are going to pay $700.00 six different ways. The Court stated that the due date for the supplements by Mr. Huysentruyt and the insurance company is the close of business on November 10, 2010.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: none present.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Continued To Oct-04-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kurt Huysentruyt for Rosie Ortiz.

Mr. Huysentruyt stated that proceeds were still coming in from the annuity, that they had not started dialogue as to how the annuity would be distributed but that he would suggest individual checks to each beneficiary (divided 6 ways), that there was no reason why the bond had not been reduced and that he was not sure as to what's on hand for distribution. The amended petition is to be filed with a hearing date of November 15, 2010. The matter was continued to October 4, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of filing of the amendment to the petition, for clarification as to what's on hand for distribution and for status of the settlement.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-23-2010 Continued To Sep-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-03-2010 Continued To Aug-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-21-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Kurt Huysentruyt appearing for petitioner. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. The Public Administator will accept this case. Mr. Huysentruyt states the reason why the petitioner filed this petitioner. She agrees to serve with bond and waive all fees. Mr. Ferdon agrees to have her appointed. Matter granted. Bond is fixed at the amount of $206,000.00. Order signed in open court. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-07-2006 Continued To Jun-21-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-17-2006 Continued To Jun-07-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
